text: Let's talk a little bit about the process of like basically what happens after that pager goes off.
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: You know, there's going to be some steps involved.
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: And I have a list of five steps here that are kind of common across the industry.
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: And I'll kind of walk through them and if there's any difference or whatever, anything to add, then we can kind of sprinkle it in here.
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: But usually when, in my experience, when that pager kind of goes off, what I've kind of noticed is businesses will go into, you know, if you have, if, again, this is important, if you don't have any type of on-call strategy or anything like that, this might be somewhere where you can kind of take some notes here.
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: The first thing that happens is you've got to triage what's going on.
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: So the page comes in, all right, what is the problem?
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: Are we getting crashes?
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: Like you said, are the login rates affected?
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: Are the signup rates affected?
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: What's going on?
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: Why did we get the page?
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: That's kind of step one, identifying what's going on.
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: So you're basically triaging the situation of what happened.
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: Step two, you kind of get into like a coordination phase of like, all right, we realize what's happened.
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: I mean, perhaps, you know, we got some crashes.
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: Now we kind of need to coordinate what's going on.
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: Maybe is this happening from the back end?
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: Is this happening from the front end?
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: Is it an update?
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: Is it a feature flag that was flipped?
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: What was going on?
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: Let's kind of get all the people on board with the situation.
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: So it's kind of coordination of what's, you know, what happened after we figured out what it was.
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: So we can kind of figure out what we need to do to fix it.
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: Then we kind of get to like step three.
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: Step three is like a mitigation phase.
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: So step three, mitigate it.
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: And now mitigation does not mean actually going through and actually fixing the problem and then shipping out a fix and all that kind of stuff.
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: Mitigation will often mean like, all right, what can we do to mitigate these errors?
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: Is there a bandaid we can apply real fast to kind of get us back on track?
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: So we can kind of come back and do this, fix this correctly.
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: Now, again, that might just be realizing, oh, we've flipped a feature flag somewhere.
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: Let's flip that off.
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: Or, hey, there's another way we can solve this.
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: Just throw this quick little hack in there to get this thing running again.
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: And so we can kind of mitigate the problem and, you know, prevent the company from bleeding a ton of money or, you know, get the signups working or something like that.
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: Step four, we get at that point after it's been mitigated and things have kind of like calmed down, you get into the resolve phase.
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: So we're going to actually resolve the actual root problem.
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: Now that could be a fix to the app.
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: We realized, oh, we were not accounting for this value to be null and it's crashing the app in this situation.
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: So they hacked it on the backend real fast, but let's go ahead and fix it finally on the front end or maybe it was some other thing.
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: At that point, you actually implement the resolution for it and maybe ship a new app update or you fix the back end or whatever the situation is and you fix it the right way.
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: And finally, you kind of full on wrap this up.
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: And I'm going through this very quickly so we can discuss this.
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: But the last thing is follow-up.
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: And that's usually going to be a lot of post-mortems of where you kind of get together.
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: And you've seen a lot of companies do this.
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: They'll say, hey, GitHub was down for four days or four hours, whatever it is.
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: And people were freaking out.
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: And they come back and say, all right, here's the post-mortem.
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: Here's what happened.
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: And they kind of walk everybody through the steps.
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: We realize the problem at this time.
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: We got together our engineers as coordination.
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: We then mitigated the risk by implementing, you know, flushing our load balancers, whatever was our mitigation.
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: Then we came back and we fixed it because it was a code problem that we'd never saw before.
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: And we wrote some tests to make sure it's never going to happen again.
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: And number five is they're kind of doing that post-mortem.
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: A lot of times these will be done behind closed doors.
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: You don't have to be public about it if your company doesn't want to.

text: But those are kind of the steps.

speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: Number two, coordinate.
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: Number three, we're going to mitigate.

text: Number four, resolve.
